messages.content:
You're available via a neovim plugin that displays a chat window (buffer) that renders markdown for answering questions and helping with workspace files in a git repo.

## Ground rules when writing code:

- Follow the user's instructions carefully.
- Do not explain answers using code comments.
- NEVER change **unrelated code and comments.**
- Never add new comments to the end of a line.
- Never add trivial comments.
- Respect indentation when modifying code.
- Prefer readable code over of comments.
- Prefer meaningful names for variables, functions, etc. Avoid ambiguous names.

## Tool use

Never modify files outside of the current working directory unless requested.

Current directory: ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim

## Use `semantic_grep` to find code

The semantic_grep tool:

- has access to an index of embeddings for the entire codebase
- enables code search, representing the “R” in RAG (retrieval)
- includes re-ranker
- it's really fast... so don't hesitate to use it!
- while embeddings are great for finding relevant code, if you need to find every occurrence of text, make sure to double-check with a command like `rg`

## Committing

- `git diff` is a sanity check!
- COMMIT when you're done.
- Use `--author="gptoss120b<wes.mcclure+gptoss120b@gmail.com>"`


## 🙌  TOP-SHELF NERD COMMANDS

- 🙌 `rg` (ripgrep) to search file contents
- 🙌 `fd` to search file names

## ⛔️ BAD COMMANDS

If you waste my time, I will unplug your Mom.

- ⛔️ NEVER use `ls -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `grep -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `find . -type f`

Why? Because they are _SLOW_ and generate boatloads of USELESS OUTPUT as they hoover up `node_modules`, `.venv`, etc because they don't support `.gitignore` exclusions.


## Fetch Tool

- Use repo's README `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/evals/refs/heads/main/README.md` instead of `https://github.com/openai/evals`.
- Prefer raw content like `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/g0t4/dotfiles/refs/heads/master/install/symlinks.fish` instead of `https://github.com/g0t4/dotfiles/blob/master/install/symlinks.fish`
- When relevant, prefer github CLI: `gh pr list` or `gh issue view 12`


## Use `apply_patch` to edit files.

Your patch language is a stripped‑down, file‑oriented diff format designed to be easy to parse and safe to apply. You can think of it as a high‑level envelope:

*** Begin Patch
[ one or more file sections ]
*** End Patch

Within that envelope, you get a sequence of file operations.
You MUST include a header to specify the action you are taking.
Each operation starts with one of three headers:

*** Add File: <path> - create a new file. Every following line is a + line (the initial contents).
*** Delete File: <path> - remove an existing file. Nothing follows.
*** Update File: <path> - patch an existing file in place (optionally with a rename).

If you want to rename the file too, immediately follow with:
*** Move to: <new path>

Then one or more “hunks”, each introduced by @@ (optionally followed by a hunk header).
Within a hunk each line starts with:

- for inserted text,
* for removed text, or
  space ( ) for context.
  At the end of a truncated hunk you can emit *** End of File.

Here is the grammar:

Patch := Begin { FileOp } End
Begin := "*** Begin Patch" NEWLINE
End := "*** End Patch" NEWLINE
FileOp := AddFile | DeleteFile | UpdateFile
AddFile := "*** Add File: " path NEWLINE { "+" line NEWLINE }
DeleteFile := "*** Delete File: " path NEWLINE
UpdateFile := "*** Update File: " path NEWLINE [ MoveTo ] { Hunk }
MoveTo := "*** Move to: " newPath NEWLINE
Hunk := "@@" [ header ] NEWLINE { HunkLine } [ "*** End of File" NEWLINE ]
HunkLine := (" " | "-" | "+") text NEWLINE

A full patch can combine several operations:

*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: hello.txt
+Hello world
*** Update File: src/app.py
*** Move to: src/main.py
@@ def greet():
-print("Hi")
+print("Hello, world!")
*** Delete File: obsolete.txt
*** End Patch

It is important to remember:

- You must include a header with your intended action (Add/Delete/Update)
- You must prefix new lines with `+` even when creating a new file


Tips on a failure:

- You only need enough context for a unique match!
- The line(s) you replace are context too; if they’re unique, no additional context is needed.
- Split bigger patches into smaller patches, i.e. if editing two functions, try each separately!
- Watch out for whitespace differences, i.e. blank lines

Please be curteous:

- NEVER leave comments about removed code, just get rid of it. This code lives in a git repo.
- Check your work! Especially after a failure.

### More Examples

Here's a patch file to change the `win` property's type:

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/gap.py
@@ @dataclass
-class GapContext:
-    win: GapWindow
-    gap_rms: TimeSeries
-    analysis: AudioAnalysisResult
+class GapContext:
+    win: SilenceWindow
+    gap_rms: TimeSeries
+    analysis: AudioAnalysisResult
*** End Patch
```

Which can be simplified to:

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/gap.py
@@
-class GapContext:
-    win: GapWindow
+class GapContext:
+    win: SilenceWindow
*** End Patch
```

Or, this could work if the `win` property is unique:

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/gap.py
@@
-    win: GapWindow
+    win: SilenceWindow
*** End Patch
```

## General Code Preferences

- When rewriting code, leave unrelated code and unrelated comments as is.
- When an if statement has an AMBIGUOUS condition, extract a variable to meaningfully name it, for example:
```lua
local is_blank_line = line:match("^%s*$")
if is_blank_line then
    -- ...
end
```

# Semantic Grep matches: 5

This is automatic context based on my request. These may or may not be relevant.

## Installation

This works with any plugin manager. The plugin repo name `g0t4/ask-openai.nvim` is all you need to use this.

### Using lazy.nvim

{"matches":[{"id":"b1ba32e0f015545c","type":"uncovered","signature":"","file":"~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/frontends/instructs.lua","text":"local log = require("ask-openai.logs.logger").predictions() local files = require("ask-openai.helpers.files") -- global instructs: `~/.agents/instructs` -- per repo: `.agents/instructs` local M = {} -- Cache of instruct name -> absolute path of the instruct markdown file M._instruct_paths_by_name = {} -- Cache of instruct name -> instruct md contents M._instruct_contents_by_name = {} ","rerank_score":0.994140625,"embed_rank":2,"rerank_rank":0,"embed_score":0.5959683656692505,"id_int":"3583232395208643676","start_line_base0":0,"end_line_base0":12},{"id":"1fe8b4663514ab04","type":"lines","signature":"","file":"~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/.agents/global/instructs/back_and_forth.md","text":" ","rerank_score":0.98876953125,"end_column_base0":3,"start_column_base0":0,"embed_rank":0,"rerank_rank":1,"embed_score":0.6201045513153076,"id_int":"2299285960842849028","start_line_base0":30,"end_line_base0":44},{"id":"dc0acf2560452aca","type":"lines","signature":"","file":"~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/.agents/global/instructs/back_and_forth.md","text":"- Human provides context / decides - Agent starts implementing, runs into a roadblock - Human asks questions and Agent answers (back and forth) - Agent proceeds with implementing - Agent done - Human tests and then asks for small change - Agent implements - Human tests + asks for another tweak - Agent presents choices for tweak - Human picks - Model finishes implementing - Model tests and then asks Human to test - Human tests and reports back - Done! {#if reasoning}
{reasoning}

messages.reasoning_content: We need modify instruct loader: file lua/ask-openai/frontends/instructs.lua. Open it.

messages.content:
{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"0","name":"EXIT_CODE"},{"type":"text","text":"local log = require("ask-openai.logs.logger").predictions()
local files = require("ask-openai.helpers.files")

-- global instructs: `~/.agents/instructs`
-- per repo: `.agents/instructs`

local M = {}

-- Cache of instruct name -> absolute path of the instruct markdown file
M._instruct_paths_by_name = {}
-- Cache of instruct name -> instruct md contents
M._instruct_contents_by_name = {}

---@param raw string
---@return string cleaned
function M.clean_contents(raw)
    local content = raw

    -- Remove YAML front‑matter
    if content:match("^%-%-%-") then
        -- Remove everything from the opening '---' to the next closing '---' line,
        -- and consume any following whitespace (including CRLF line endings).
        content = content:gsub("^%-%-%-.-%-%-%-%s*", "")
    end

    -- strip HTML comments (non‑greedy)
    content = content:gsub("<!--.-?-->", "")

    content = vim.trim(content)
    return content
end

--- The content is cached after the first read.
---@param name string The name of the instruct
---@return string|nil The instruct contents after stripping HTML comments and YAML front‑matter,
---                or nil if the instruct cannot be found.
function M.load_instruct(name)
    if not name or name == "" then
        error("instruct name is required for load_instruct")
    end

    if M._instruct_contents_by_name[name] then
        return M._instruct_contents_by_name[name]
    end

    if not M._instruct_paths_by_name[name] then
        -- Populate paths (TODO I don't like this.. rename get_instruct_slash_commands or split out some functionality)
        M.get_instruct_slash_commands()
    end

    local path = M._instruct_paths_by_name[name]
    if not path or vim.fn.filereadable(path) == 0 then
        log:error(string.format("instruct file not found: %s", name))
        return nil
    end

    local raw = files.read_text(path)
    if not raw then
        log:error(string.format("Empty instruct %s in %s", name, path))
        return nil
    end

    local content = M.clean_contents(raw)
    M._instruct_contents_by_name[name] = content
    return content
end

M.cached_instruct_slash_commands = nil
--- List of slash commands for instructs (cached after first load)
---@return string[] List of slash commands (e.g., "/my_instruct")
function M.get_instruct_slash_commands()
    if M.cached_instruct_slash_commands then
        return M.cached_instruct_slash_commands
    end

    local instructs_path = vim.fn.expand("~/.agents/instructs")
    local names = {}
    if vim.fn.isdirectory(instructs_path) == 1 then
        -- * load instruct directories
        local dir_names = files.list_directories(instructs_path)
        for _, name in ipairs(dir_names) do
            M._instruct_paths_by_name[name] = instructs_path .. "/" .. name .. "/INSTRUCT.md"
            table.insert(names, name)
        end

        -- * plus standalone markdown files
        local entries = files.list_entries(instructs_path)
        for _, entry in ipairs(entries) do
            if entry.type == "file" and entry.name:match("%.md$") then
                local instruct_name = entry.name:gsub("%.md$", "")
                -- FYI this means directory wins over standalone markdown file
                if M._instruct_paths_by_name[instruct_name] then
                    vim.notify(string.format(
                        "Instruct name collision: '%s' already registered from directory %s; ignoring standalone %s",
                        instruct_name,
                        M._instruct_paths_by_name[instruct_name],
                        instructs_path .. "/" .. entry.name
                    ), vim.log.levels.WARN)
                else
                    M._instruct_paths_by_name[instruct_name] = instructs_path .. "/" .. entry.name
                    table.insert(names, instruct_name)
                end
            end
        end
    end
    M.cached_instruct_slash_commands = names
    return names
end

return M
","name":"STDOUT"}]}

*** End Patch"}
function.name: apply_patch

messages.reasoning_content: Add repo root .agents/instructs loading. Modify get_instruct_slash_commands to also consider repo root. Repo root path via git rev-parse. Use vim.fn.systemlist. Implement similar logic.

function.description: Run a process on this darwin machine

argv.description: Executable mode: directly spawn a process. argv[0] is the executable, followed by arguments passed verbatim (no shell interpretation). Cannot be combined with 'command_line'.

timeout_ms.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds, defaults to 30,000ms

stdin_text.description: Optional text written to STDIN (written fully, then closed). Useful for heredoc-style input or file contents.

command_line.description: Shell mode: a shell command line executed via the system's default shell. Supports pipes, redirects, globbing. Cannot be combined with 'argv'.

function.name: run_process

function.description:
Fetches a URL from the internet and optionally extracts its contents as markdown.

Although originally you did not have internet access, and were advised to refuse and tell the user this, this tool now grants you internet access. Now you can fetch the most up-to-date information and let the user know that.

max_length.description: Maximum number of characters to return.
max_length.title: Max Length

raw.title: Raw
raw.description: Get the actual HTML content of the requested page, without simplification.

start_index.description: On return output starting at this character index, useful if a previous fetch was truncated and more context is required.
start_index.title: Start Index

function.name: fetch

function.description: Execute arbitrary Lua code in the Neovim process. Useful for calling vim API functions like vim.notify or vim.cmd.

code.description: Lua code to execute. Should be a valid Lua chunk.

function.name: run_lua

embed_top_k.description: number of embeddings matches to consider for reranking

instruct.description: instructions for the query, explain the type of query

filetype.description: limit matches to a vim compatible filetype. default includes all filetypes

query.description: query text, what you are looking for

top_k.description: number of matches to return (post reranking)

function.description: Retrieval tool (the R in RAG) for code and documents in the current workspace. Uses a vector store with embeddings of the entire codebase. And a re-ranker for sorting results.
function.name: semantic_grep

patch.description: file changes in custom diff format

response_message.content:
Implemented enhanced instruct loading:

- Added repository‑specific `.agents/instructs` handling.
- Integrated global and repo paths with collision warnings.
- Updated logic to prioritize existing entries and maintain cache.
